pullRequest.title: REF: refactor dataframe plotting
pullRequest.body:
xref #3689 

This PR refactors our plotting implmeentation for dataframes. It builds upon #3708 and changes a lot if internals in an attempt to 1) make sure the code is more maintainable, 2) allow additional flexibility and features for users, 3) fix some long-standing bugs.

You will be able to see it in the diff but some notable changes:

- Categorical plotting works differently now. Each category-geom type pair is treated as a single collection, added together with a label to the Axis. Thanks to the work on collections in #3708, this now means that matplotlib can generate the legend on its own, with proper and complete styiing of handles, including geometry types.
- You can now map colors and other styles to categories via dictionaries. The result will, once again, automatically shows up in legend. No need to figure our array of colors beforehand.
- Choropleths defined with the `scheme` keyword now have two different implemtations, for backwards compatibility. By default, we still treat it as a categorical plot, though with different internal implementation. Alternatively, you can pass `legend_kwds={"colorbar": True}` in which case we use the bins from mapclassify to define `norm` and generate a discrete colorbar with the plot being passed through numeric plotting. Ideally, the colorbar solution would become default in 2.0 but this is for a discussion. Note that the `spacing` keyword can then control the spacing of ticks. That is equal to `scale` keyword in `legend_kwds` in `explore`.
- vmin and vmax is supported when `scheme` is defined, not ignored.
- When using categorical colormap with categorical plots, we now iterate in order, resulting in optimal contrast (e.g. you get blue and orange for binary plot, not blue and light blue).
- Colorbar size is automatically adjusted to be more or less the size of the plot if needed.
- Colrobar now indicates extensions if the vmin, vmax clips the actual values.
- We do not create the legend on our own. At all times, it is matplotlib which deals with all of it as we always properly register all collections with labels and supported handles. This also means that you can create a single legend from a layered map by calling `ax.legend()` directly.

It closes quite a few of things:

Issues

Closes #2208
Closes #2154
Closes #1741
Closes #1645
Closes #1621
Closes #1269
Closes #983
Closes #735
Closes #660

PRs superseded by this:

Closes #2012 
Closes #2239
Closes #2970
Closes #2853

Once merged, there will be a follow-up focusing on documentation. More stuff will be added to the user guide outlining this while some examples will be cleaned and updated. It may be that I'll also find some issues during that :).

There's also some other stuff I'd like to do, like pulling the units from the CRS in the style of xarray or [xvec](https://xvec.readthedocs.io/en/stable/plotting.html), expose contextily through tiles keyword to match explore and expose greedy coloring via mapclassify. But those are delimited and will be done as follow up PRs.

While this is mostly backwards compatible from a user perspective, as is the maps will look the same (mostly), it changes the internal structure of the Axes in terms of collections. So anything relying on that will likely break.

pullRequest.title: ENH: support missing `properties` key in `from_features`
pullRequest.body:
Hi! This fixes #2506, an old bug that resulted in a `KeyError` when running `from_features` on data without the `properties` field. I have also updated an old test to be clear that empty or `null` `properties` do not produce any column.

Let me know if anything needs to be adjusted.

pullRequest.title: DOC: clarify sindex.query behavior with predicates
pullRequest.body:
## Summary

Fixes #2668

The previous docstring for `SpatialIndex.query` implied that only bounding boxes are used for matching, which is misleading when a predicate is provided.

## Changes

Clarified the behavior at the start of the docstring:

- **Without predicate**: Returns geometries whose bounding box intersects the bounding box of the input geometry
- **With predicate**: First filters by bounding box intersection, then applies the predicate using the **actual geometry** (not the envelope)

This helps users understand that when they use predicates like "intersects" or "contains", the exact geometry is used for the spatial test, not the bounding box.

pullRequest.title: BUG: inserting rows into empty geodataframe should preserve CRS
pullRequest.body:
Closes #3109, extension of #3741. This actually changes behaviour on our side so best to leave split. Diff will be cleaner once that is merged.

Also fairly sure I'll have to go back and fix old pandas versions.

pullRequest.title: COMPAT: fix failing test for loc concatenation crs preservation with pandas 3.1
pullRequest.body:
Closes #3119 -- but I didn't put in the changelog because it will only close with pandas 3.1... not sure how best to handle that.

This is a consequence of https://github.com/pandas-dev/pandas/pull/62523 which has had main failing for the last little while.

This is a nice change though, upstream changes actually fixing bugs for us. I've just updated the test for right now, but we could also look into smoothing over the warning that gets produced - internally within the new pandas call we hit this function

```python
def infer_and_maybe_downcast(orig: ExtensionArray, new_arr) -> ArrayLike:  
    new_arr = orig._cast_pointwise_result(new_arr)  
  
    dtype = orig.dtype  
    if isinstance(dtype, NumpyEADtype):  
        # error: Incompatible types in assignment (expression has  
        # type "dtype[Any]", variable has type "ExtensionDtype")        # [assignment]        dtype = dtype.numpy_dtype  # type: ignore[assignment]  
  
    if is_np_dtype(new_arr.dtype, "f") and is_np_dtype(dtype, "iu"):  
        new_arr = maybe_downcast_to_dtype(new_arr, dtype)  
    return new_arr
```

so to preserve the CRS through the concat withou the warning, we could possibly override `_cast_pointwise_result` or `_from_scalars` - or we could also move the CRS into the dtype (which might have been talked about a while back, but is likely more work, and maybe not so helpful with mutliple geometry engines)

This also fixes https://github.com/geopandas/geopandas/issues/3119 which I'm quite happy to see, I had no idea how to resolve that from our side.

pullRequest.title: add opportunistic range request
pullRequest.body:
Closes #3571 

In `read_file(url)`, opportunistically tries a range request and only downloads the whole file if range requests are explicitly not supported (`"Accept-Ranges": "none"` in the header) or if the status is not `206` (PARTIAL_CONTENT).

This makes it possible to avoid downloading large files if they can be read partially with range requests but the server doesn't explicitly advertise it with the header `"Accept-Ranges": "bytes"`.

See the issue for a further description.

pullRequest.title: DOC: Move `active_geometry_name` change to version 1.0.0
pullRequest.body:
I was trying to figure out which version active_geometry_name was introduced. In the changelog, the change fell under 0.14.0, but it wasn't there when I used that version. Looking at the original [PR](https://github.com/geopandas/geopandas/pull/2943), it was originally meant to go in 0.14, but eventually ended up in 1.0.0, but the changelog wasn't updated. This PR updates the change log.

Closes #3618

pullRequest.title: ENH: Expose all geopy services
pullRequest.body:
This uses geopy's `get_geocoder_for_service` to map strings to the proper class
to use, so a geopandas user can use any available geopy Geocoder. This also
provides the option to use a Geocoder instance, rather than the string
mapping, which is potentially more transparent. I'll add a couple inline notes on
potentially questionable changes.

Closes #468 and closes #539.

pullRequest.title: CI: run tests also on python 3.14
pullRequest.body:
Some optional dependencies are not available yet for python 3.14, so are commented out for now:
- fiona: https://github.com/conda-forge/fiona-feedstock/pull/237

Now 3.14 is available on the main channel, readthedocs doesn't seem to be able to create the conda environment anymore because some pip packages are giving issues... so I pinned 3.13 there.

closes: #3657
